title: Scroll Reveal, Swiss Grid, Handwriting, Dock, Segmented
description: Pure-CSS scroll reveals, Swiss editorial grid, SVG handwriting draw-on, CSS :has() dock magnification, and spring segmented controls.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Velocity Reveals
drops.description: Pure CSS cards that animate in as they cross the viewport — zero JavaScript, using animation-timeline: view().
drops.concept: The W3C Scroll-Driven Animations spec lets elements animate in response to their own visibility in the viewport. Using animation-timeline: view() and animation-range: entry 0% entry 35%, each card fades and rises from below as it enters the scroll port. Stagger is achieved via nth-child selectors with offset ranges — no IntersectionObserver, no JS.
drops.source: W3C Scroll-Driven Animations Spec / Chrome Team

interaction.mouse: Scroll down to see cards animate in

drops.title: Swiss Editorial Grid
drops.description: 12-column asymmetric newspaper layout with deliberate overlap zones and Swiss typographic hierarchy.
drops.concept: Swiss International Typographic Style (grid + red accent + bold type hierarchy) applied to a CSS Grid 12-column layout. The key technique is using negative margins inside a grid to create deliberate overlapping zones — elements that 'break' the grid to signal importance. Red (#CC0000) as the single accent color echoes mid-century editorial authority.
drops.source: Swiss International Typographic Style / Josef Müller-Brockmann

interaction.mouse: Hover cards for subtle background shift

drops.title: SVG Handwriting Stroke Reveal
drops.description: Five variants of the stroke-dashoffset draw-on effect — from classic serif to neon glow.
drops.concept: SVG text elements become animatable when fill is set to none and stroke-dasharray + pathLength are used with animation. By setting pathLength: 1 on the element, stroke-dasharray: 1 creates a single normalized dash, and animating stroke-dashoffset from 1 to 0 draws the text. Five demos cover monochrome, chromatic stagger, stroke-then-fill reveal, architectural outline, and glowing neon variants.
drops.source: Codrops Animated Text Fills tutorial / SVGator blog

interaction.mouse: Click Replay buttons to restart animations

drops.title: CSS :has() Dock Magnification
drops.description: MacOS dock-style icon magnification built entirely with CSS :has() and nth-child selectors — zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: The :has() relational pseudo-class (now baseline in all major browsers) lets us query 'does this container have a hovered child?' — then use nth-child to target specific siblings and scale them proportionally based on distance from the hovered item. Combined with cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) spring easing, this recreates the macOS dock magnification effect with pure CSS. Three dock variants demonstrate different visual styles.
drops.source: frontendfyi / macOS dock animation with CSS :has()

interaction.mouse: Hover over dock icons to see magnification cascade

drops.title: Morphing Segmented Controls
drops.description: Five segmented control variants with a spring-physics sliding indicator and live content preview.
drops.concept: Segmented controls (the pill-shaped tab switcher made famous by iOS) get a physics-driven upgrade: the active indicator slides between options using cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) spring easing, with its left position and width both transitioning simultaneously. Five visual variants (glass, glow, light, neon, icon-tab) prove the pattern works across design languages. Each selection updates a content preview panel, demonstrating real utility beyond aesthetics.

interaction.mouse: Click segments to slide the indicator
